steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The clause before the blank (“Metamorphoses, an epic poem of Roman origin, dates back to the [BLANK]”) is an independent clause. The clause after (“the poem's heroic narrative still captivates modern readers”) is also an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content:
To join two independent clauses, use a comma followed by a coordinating conjunction. “Yet” is a coordinating conjunction that expresses the contrast between the poem's ancient origin and its modern appeal.

The wording “1st century, yet” applies this structure.

Two alternatives lack the conjunction entirely, while the remaining alternative misplaces the comma.

That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
